Closing as resolved.","status":"closed","title":"review: CLI, continuity, and usage (AR-2026-04-12)","updated_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}
{"acceptance":"1. `ProviderEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint providers via model catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 providers at different prices for the same model and asserts correct population.
3. Subscription harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each harness + each curve band.
4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 providers with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 providers with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).
5. Unknown-cost providers are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost providers are not starved, unknown-cost providers are not demoted into last place.
6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent harness with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: "unknown"`.
7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude harness at 92% quota, seed an openrouter provider with equivalent model at known cost → openrouter wins.
8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which provider was picked and why.
9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).
10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.","claimed-at":"2026-04-22T22:34:06Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2792459

"

data.text: This bead is substantially larger than a single execute-bead pass should attempt (10 ACs spanning breaking config changes, refactors across 5+ files, Playwright goldens, regression fixtures, and dead-code sweep). The right move per the bead instructions is a justified no_changes with a clear decomposition recommendation rather than a partial commit.
